module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency forward-looking — arc-naming as agent-mirror move

rules_evaluated.evidence: One registry-expanded prompt (arc-retrospective-1) with full forcing function: walk artefacts, write arc, quote files, no template essay.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write — but downstream reader exists here.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body line 25-27 makes the save optional ('If the note reads true, save it. You'll have your agent re-read it when the next long task stalls.') Downstream reader: the agent on next long task. (a) consumer named per check_prompts rule 27.
Body line 15: 'A fresh session, so there's no scrollback coloring the read.' Fresh-session is the stronger fix (per rule 31).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get body-side recovery callout.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body line 19: 'Claude will likely open with a plan-summary paragraph... Skim past the opening; the note is what you're reading for.' Body line 21: 'If the read runs ten minutes plus, interrupt with enough. just tell me now.' — recovery move encoded.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Name the consumer at write time.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Save destination consumed by 'agent re-reads when next long task stalls' — (a) downstream agent reader. Optional save, with clear (b) Debrief-recall use named.
One marker with one-sentence action lead-in (line 16: 'Ask the agent to walk them and write the arc from them').
Body verbs: read, walk, write, push back, name. No defend / justify / prove framing.
Maintainer block line 39: 'practitioner fluency (the module's mood). The arc-naming is forward-looking, not nostalgic.'
Maintainer block line 45: 'Agent-as-mirror — lets the artefacts do the talking, not the trainer.' Strategy doc names this for M6 close.
Hands to Debrief + closer chain. M7/M8 territory not pre-empted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment — note is a reading, not a verdict.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 56: 'Note should feel like a reading, not a verdict. If it reads pass/fail, the frame slipped.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — strategy throughline 'scaling of learning.'
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body line 6-7: 'The throughline of the training is that everything is scaling of learning. Each module's loop is the same loop at a wider scope. That's only visible retrospectively.'
